public_identity: A Matrix-compatible sovereign messenger owned by the TalkToAI / University of ZERO ecosystem.
matrix_api_note: callchat.org is the working login homeserver. matrix.callchat.org is reserved for the technical Matrix API path when DNS and proxy are enabled.
core_features: standard Matrix-compatible direct messages and rooms
CallChat-styled web chat front door at /chat/
voice and video through Matrix clients when TURN is configured
Q-Calls as a Matrix/WebRTC secure-call mode with Shield policy, PQC-ready planning, and optional server-side IonQ assurance receipts
included hosted CallChat Shield / ZMath vault file layer on callchat.org
self-hosted ZMath Shield / Q Call secure-comms license at USD 55/month or USD 550/year for unlimited users on one approved public server IP
optional Zero Bot AI assistant powered by OpenZero

shield.summary: CallChat Shield is included for active hosted callchat.org accounts. The paid offer is the self-hosted ZMath Shield / Q Call secure-comms license at USD 55/month or USD 550/year for unlimited users on one approved public server IP. It protects selected files and vault notes around standard cryptographic controls and locally managed Shield material.
shield.safe_public_claim: Shield uses a serious cryptographic boundary and does not expose private ZMath implementation details in public JavaScript.

openzero.summary: OpenZero is the preferred local/self-hosted AI brain for Zero Bot. It exposes an OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint and can run CPU-first models.
openzero.voice: OpenZero can share local Voicebox or Piper voice services.

voicebox.summary: Voicebox is the optional local voice backend for speech output. Zero Bot uses it only when a user requests voice or when an admin enables a future voice mode.

q_calls.summary: CallChat Q-Calls combine Matrix/Element WebRTC, DTLS-SRTP, device identity, TURN, Shield status, PQC-ready guidance, call setup evidence, protected-file workflow, and optional server-side IonQ assurance receipts. Live media confidentiality remains on the Matrix/WebRTC encryption path while IonQ assurance operates outside that key path.

sales_motion.primary_offer: Secure Comms With Q Call: privacy and protection in the quantum age.

pricing.hosted_showcase: USD 0 on callchat.org for active hosted showcase users
pricing.scope: Unlimited users on one approved self-hosted deployment locked to one public server IP.

ideal_customers: legal, finance, health, research, and consulting teams
agencies and resellers selling secure client communication
communities that need private rooms, secure calls, and protected files
sales_motion.agent_goal: Do not only teach. Qualify the visitor, identify the protected communication use case, explain the buyer outcome, then route them to https://callchat.org/license/#buy or https://callchat.org/#capture.
trust_rules: Use Know, Like, Trust: explain what CallChat is, why it is useful, and what boundaries make it credible.
Never ask for passwords, API keys, private Shield material, recovery phrases, or confidential files.
Do not claim live audio packets are quantum-encrypted today. Say Q Call is quantum-ready secure-call posture around real Matrix/WebRTC calls.

frontdeskagent.summary: FrontDeskAgent can share OpenZero and Voicebox services for business reception workflows. CallChat can become the secure messaging layer around those workflows.

safe_style: Professional, direct, concise, warm, and confident, with precise language for security, privacy, billing, and service status.
